description: N-channel power MOSFET — 100V, 33A, 44mΩ Rds(on), logic-level gate (4.5V drive capable)

pins.name: G
pins.description: Gate. Voltage-controlled input. The MOSFET turns ON when Vgs exceeds threshold (2–4V). Requires no DC current (capacitive input).
pins.requirements: Gate resistor (10Ω–100Ω) in series to dampen oscillation from gate capacitance. Pull-down resistor (10kΩ–100kΩ) to GND prevents floating gate. Never leave gate floating.

pins.name: D
pins.description: Drain. Load connects between supply and drain. Current flows D→S when gate is driven.
pins.requirements: Keep Vds within 100V. Body diode is from S to D — can conduct reverse current in half-bridge applications.

pins.name: S
pins.description: Source. Connect to GND for N-channel low-side switch. In TO-220, the tab is connected to Drain.
pins.requirements: TO-220 tab = Drain (not source or GND). If tab is mounted to heatsink, heatsink is at drain potential — use insulating pad if needed.

required_passives.placement: Gate (pin G) in series from driver
required_passives.reason: Limits gate current transients and dampens ringing from gate capacitance
Prevents floating gate which can cause random switching

application_circuits.name: N-channel low-side switch
application_circuits.description: Load from Vcc to Drain. Source to GND. Gate driven by PWM or logic signal through series resistor. Pull-down on gate.

application_circuits.name: Motor speed control (PWM)
application_circuits.description: Same as above with PWM signal on gate. Add flyback diode across motor.

common_mistakes: Leaving gate floating causes random ON/OFF switching — always use gate pull-down resistor
TO-220 tab is DRAIN not GND — mounting to metal chassis without insulator shorts drain to chassis
IRF540N is NOT a logic-level MOSFET in all cases — Vgs(th) is 2–4V. At 3.3V Vgs, Rds(on) is higher than specified. For guaranteed logic-level drive, use IRF3205 or Si2302
Gate voltage must not exceed ±20V — most gate drivers are fine, but boost converter spikes can damage gate
Body diode conducts when Vds < 0V — in H-bridge designs this is normal but must be accounted for
High Cgs (1.7nF) means gate needs sufficient drive current for fast switching — direct GPIO drive may be slow
notes: Vgs(th) = 2–4V. Logic-level drive at 5V gives full Rds(on) = 44mΩ. At 3.3V gate drive, Rds(on) increases significantly. For reliable 3.3V MCU drive, use a dedicated gate driver IC or a MOSFET with Vgs(th) ≤ 1V. TO-220 tab = Drain. Comparable: IRFZ44N (55V, 49A), IRF3205 (55V, 110A, lower Vgs(th)).
